title: Starting a Statistical Conversation Across Disciplines
text:
Statistics becomes especially powerful when it helps people from different disciplines ask better questions together. In many collaborative settings, the technical challenge is only one part of the work. Another equally important part is building a shared language around uncertainty, evidence, assumptions, and interpretation.
In my experience, the most productive statistical collaborations begin when methods are discussed not only in terms of model choice, but also in terms of the decisions they are meant to inform. This creates room for richer conversations and for methodology to stay connected to the real-world problem at hand.
This blog will be a place to share reflections like these, along with short notes on teaching, data analysis, and statistical practice.

title: Teaching
text:
Patricia’s teaching integrates mathematical and statistical rigour with reproducible practice and applied problem-solving. She has extensive experience in course design, ranging from large first-year classes to specialist master’s programs. Her approach emphasises inclusive and effective learning environments that connect academic concepts with the skills required in industry and government, preparing students for real-world challenges.
Experience in Leadership in Teaching and Learning: Director of Education, Department of Econometrics and Business Statistics, Monash University
From November 2022 to February 2024, I held a leadership position in teaching and learning within the department, which concluded when I moved to the University of Melbourne.

title: Teaching
text:
Teaching Experience

University of Melbourne, Australia

MAST10010, Introduction to Data Analysis (Chief Examiner) Undergraduate level. Updated all subject materials using Quarto reproducible files and transitioned the course from Minitab to R, including redevelopment of all materials and creation of a new Canvas LMS with custom HTML pages.
MAST20005, Statistics (Lecturer), undergraduate level, 2024. Developed detailed annotated lecture slides, highly praised in student teaching evaluations.
MAST90058 (Lecturer), master level, 2024. Developed detailed annotated lecture slides, highly praised in student teaching evaluations.



Monash University, Australia

ETC5513, Collaborative and Reproducible Practice (Chief Examiner), master level, 2020-2023. Created the unit for the Master of Business Analytics and received purple letters from the Monash Business School Dean for outstanding teaching and learning.
ETC5510, Introduction to Data Analysis (Chief Examiner), master level, from 2021. Adapted materials, created new teaching resources, and received purple letters from the Monash Business School Dean.
ETC1010, Introduction to Data Analysis (Chief Examiner), undergraduate level, 2020-2022. Adapted materials, created new teaching resources
ETC5512, Wild Caught Data (Lecturer, 25% of the unit), master level, 2020 Received a blue letter from the Monash Business School Dean.



The University of Queensland, Australia

STAT3003, Experimental Design (Lecturer), third-year undergraduate, 2013-2014
STAT4404, Advanced Stochastic Process (Course Coordinator), fourth-year honours, 2013-2014
STAT1201, Analysis of Scientific Data (Course Coordinator), first year, 2013-2014 Taught a cohort of more than 800 students and received a commendation from the Head of the School of Mathematics and Physics.



Public University of Navarre, Spain

Statistics for Engineers I (Course Coordinator), first-year undergraduate, 2010-2011
Statistical Methods for Engineers (Lecturer), third-year undergraduate, 2010-2011
Statistics I (Lecturer), first-year undergraduate in Business and Economics, 2010-2011

title: Teaching
text:
R, RStudio and RStudio Cloud

2024: Inequality analysis using R: Disaggregated data from surveys Inequality analysis using R: Disaggregated data from surveys was produced by the Health Equity Monitoring Team in the Division of Data, Analytics and Delivery for Impact at the World Health Organization (WHO): Ahmad Reza Hosseinpoor, Katherine Kirkby, Andreia Baptista, Nicole Bergen, Daniel Antiporta and Joshua Mutio. Dr Patricia Menéndez (School of Mathematics and Statistics, University of Melbourne) was a main contributor to the development of the course materials.
2022: Posit invited YouTube live lecture, An inclusive solution for teaching and learning R during the COVID pandemic
2021: RStudio Cloud: An inclusive solution for learning R Official Posit guest post by Patricia Menéndez on using RStudio Cloud to teach large classes and reduce setup barriers during remote learning.
2022: Teaching Data Science in the Cloud Posit post summarising a live webinar series with Patricia Menéndez, Brian Anderson, Mine Çetinkaya-Rundel, and Pete Knast, focused on teaching with RStudio Cloud and scaling to larger classes.

title: Research
text: My research examines how digital systems mediate public life. I focus on the institutional settings where technology moves from prototype to policy, and on the social consequences of that transition.

title: Research
text: My research examines how digital systems mediate public life. I focus on the institutional settings where technology moves from prototype to policy, and on the social consequences of that transition.

title: Research
text:
Current Projects

Governing AI in Social Services
This project studies how agencies deploy predictive and classification systems in eligibility, compliance, and case-management workflows. It combines interviews, policy analysis, and audit-oriented evaluation methods.


Civic Data Infrastructures
A comparative program of research on how municipalities build shared digital platforms for transportation, planning, and resident services, with particular attention to legitimacy and accountability.


Participatory Technology Assessment
An ongoing collaboration with community organizations to design practical, accessible ways for non-technical stakeholders to evaluate public-sector technologies.

title: Research
text:
Methods

Mixed methods research design
Interview-based fieldwork
Computational text analysis
Institutional analysis
Participatory design workshops

title: Research
text:
Collaboration
I welcome conversations about interdisciplinary collaborations, invited talks, and graduate supervision related to technology governance, public policy, and civic systems.

title: Fun
text: Outside of work, I love carving wooden spoons, especially from green wood. I enjoy working with white birch, olive, cherry, and fruit woods, so if you have any cuttings, let me know!

title: Home
text:
Senior Lecturer in Statistics and Data Science in the School of Mathematics and Statistics at the University of Melbourne, Australia.


 Email  Google Scholar  ORCID  GitHub  Bluesky













Profile photo




Senior Lecturer in Statistics and Data Science
School of Mathematics and Statistics
The University of Melbourne, Australia




Statistical methods for time series
Computational statistics
Machine learning
Data visualisation tools
Data science
Statistical education


Email
patricia.menendez@unimelb.edu.au







Educated in Spain, Japan and Switzerland, Patricia Menéndez is a Senior Lecturer in Statistics and Data Science at the School of Mathematics and Statistics at the University of Melbourne, Australia.
Patricia’s training is in mathematics and Statistics and she received her PhD from the Mathematics Department at ETH Zurich in Switzerland. Since completing her PhD she has held academic positions at the Public University of the Navarre, University of New South Wales, University of Queensland and Monash University. Before joining the University of Melbourne, she has also worked outside academia as statistician for the NSW Bureau of Crime Statistics and Research, and for the Australian Institute of Marine Science.
Patricia has experience working on multidisciplinary projects to answer research and policy-making questions in the fields of climate change, environmental and marine sciences besides criminology. She has expertise developing and applying statistical methodology and computational methods as well as providing statistical training. She is very passionate about the application and development of statistical methodology to solve real life problems and bringing statistical knowledge closer to other disciplines.
Her research interests include statistical methods for time series, computational statistics, machine learning, data visualisation tools and data science. She is also an enthusiastic educator.




2009 PhD, ETH Zurich, Switzerland
2004 Master’s Course (Research Student), Environmental Synthesis and Analysis Graduate School of Natural Science and Technology, Okayama University, Japan Monbukagakusho (MEXT) Scholarship Recipient
2002 Licenciate in Mathematics, University of Valladolid, Spain




2020 Foundations for Effective Teaching, Monash University Education Academy, Australia
2013 Certificate in Higher Education, Design Learning for Higher Education, University of Queensland, Australia
2013 Certificate in Higher Education, Design Teaching and Assessment, University of Queensland, Australia










Methodological and applied work focused on modelling complex temporal data and extracting meaningful structure from dynamic systems.



Research on statistical computation, modern machine learning methods, and practical tools for analysing real-world data.



Developing accessible analytical workflows and visualisation approaches that help bring statistical knowledge closer to other disciplines.
